briefdescription.para: Parses a 2-digits integer and updates num with the value.

detaileddescription.para: cur is updated to point just after the integer. In case of error, invalid is set to TRUE, values of num and cur are undefined.

memberdef.name: PARSE_FLOAT
memberdef.initializer: PARSE_2_DIGITS(num, cur, invalid); \ if (!invalid && (*cur == '.')) { \ double mult = 1; \ cur++; \ if ((*cur < '0') || (*cur > '9')) \ invalid = 1; \ while ((*cur >= '0') && (*cur <= '9')) { \ mult /= 10; \ num += (*cur - '0') * mult; \ cur++; \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: Parses a float and updates num with the value.

detaileddescription.para: cur is updated to point just after the float. The float must have a 2-digits integer part and may or may not have a decimal part. In case of error, invalid is set to TRUE, values of num and cur are undefined.

briefdescription.para: Parses a digits integer and updates num with the value.

detaileddescription.para: cur is updated to point just after the integer. In case of error, num_type is set to -1, values of num and cur are undefined.

memberdef.name: PARSE_NUM
memberdef.initializer: num = 0; \ PARSE_DIGITS(num, cur, num_type); \ if (!num_type && (*cur == '.')) { \ double mult = 1; \ cur++; \ if ((*cur < '0') || (*cur > '9')) \ num_type = -1; \ else \ num_type = 1; \ while ((*cur >= '0') && (*cur <= '9')) { \ mult /= 10; \ num += (*cur - '0') * mult; \ cur++; \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: Parses a float or integer and updates num with the value.

detaileddescription.para: cur is updated to point just after the number. If the number is a float, then it must have an integer part and a decimal part; num_type will be set to 1. If there is no decimal part, num_type is set to zero. In case of error, num_type is set to -1, values of num and cur are undefined.

memberdef.name: RETURN_TYPE_IF_VALID
memberdef.initializer: if (IS_TZO_CHAR(*cur)) { \ ret = _xmlSchemaParseTimeZone(&(dt->value.date), &cur); \ if (ret == 0) { \ if (*cur != 0) \ goto error; \ dt->type = t; \ goto done; \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: Calculates the number of seconds in the time portion of dt.

simplesect.para: seconds.

briefdescription.para: Handle an out of memory condition.
Allocate a new simple type value.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new value or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: brief Compares two decimals

detaileddescription.para: param lhs param rhs return positive value if lhs > rhs, negative if lhs < rhs, or 0 if lhs == rhs

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the default XML Schemas type library.
Initialize the default XML Schemas type library.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the default XML Schemas type library.

xrefdescription.para: This function will be made private. Call xmlCleanupParser to free global state but see the warnings there. xmlCleanupParser should be only called once at program exit. In most cases, you don't have to call cleanup functions at all.

briefdescription.para: Evaluates if a specific facet can be used in conjunction with a type.

simplesect.para: 1 if the facet can be used with the given built-in type, 0 otherwise and -1 in case the type is not a built-in type.

briefdescription.para: Gives you the type struct for a built-in type by its type id.

parameterdescription.para: the type of the built in type

simplesect.para: the type if found, NULL otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Appends a next sibling to a list of computed values.

simplesect.para: 0 if succeeded and -1 on API errors.

briefdescription.para: Accessor for the next sibling of a list of computed values.

simplesect.para: the next value or NULL if there was none, or on API errors.

briefdescription.para: Accessor for the string value of a computed value.

simplesect.para: the string value or NULL if there was none, or on API errors.

briefdescription.para: Accessor for the boolean value of a computed value.

simplesect.para: 1 if true and 0 if false, or in case of an error. Hmm.

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new simple type value.

detaileddescription.para: The type can be of XML_SCHEMAS_STRING. WARNING: This one is intended to be expanded for other string based types. We need this for anySimpleType as well. The given value is consumed and freed with the struct.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new value or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new NOTATION value.

detaileddescription.para: The given values are consumed and freed with the struct.

parameterdescription.para: the notation namespace name or NULL

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new value or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new QName value.

detaileddescription.para: The given values are consumed and freed with the struct.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new value or NULL in case of an error.

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the default XML Schemas type library.
Lookup a type in the default XML Schemas type library.

parameterdescription.para: the URI of the namespace usually "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"

simplesect.para: the type if found, NULL otherwise

briefdescription.para: Lookup function.

simplesect.para: the item type of type as defined by the built-in datatype hierarchy of XML Schema Part 2: Datatypes, or NULL in case of an error.

briefdescription.para: Parses a xs:gYear without time zone and fills in the appropriate field of the dt structure.

detaileddescription.para: str is updated to point just after the xs:gYear. It is supposed that dt->year is big enough to contain the year.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

simplesect.para: 0 or the error code

briefdescription.para: Parses a xs:gMonth without time zone and fills in the appropriate field of the dt structure.

detaileddescription.para: str is updated to point just after the xs:gMonth.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

simplesect.para: 0 or the error code

briefdescription.para: Parses a xs:gDay without time zone and fills in the appropriate field of the dt structure.

detaileddescription.para: str is updated to point just after the xs:gDay.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

simplesect.para: 0 or the error code

briefdescription.para: Parses a xs:time without time zone and fills in the appropriate fields of the dt structure.

detaileddescription.para: str is updated to point just after the xs:time. In case of error, values of dt fields are undefined.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

simplesect.para: 0 or the error code

briefdescription.para: Parses a time zone without time zone and fills in the appropriate field of the dt structure.

detaileddescription.para: str is updated to point just after the time zone.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

simplesect.para: 0 or the error code

briefdescription.para: Converts a base64 encoded character to its base 64 value.

simplesect.para: 0-63 (value), 64 (pad), or -1 (not recognized)

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaValType type, const xmlChar *dateTime, xmlSchemaValPtr *val, int collapse)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateDates

briefdescription.para: Check that dateTime conforms to the lexical space of one of the date types.

detaileddescription.para: if true a value is computed and returned in val.

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaTypePtr type, const xmlChar *duration, xmlSchemaValPtr *val, int collapse)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateDuration

briefdescription.para: Check that duration conforms to the lexical space of the duration type.

detaileddescription.para: if true a value is computed and returned in val.

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Removes the leading and ending spaces of a string.

simplesect.para: the new string or NULL if no change was required.

briefdescription.para: Replaces 0xd, 0x9 and 0xa with a space.

simplesect.para: the new string or NULL if no change was required.

briefdescription.para: Removes and normalize white spaces in the string.

simplesect.para: the new string or NULL if no change was required.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaTypePtr type, const xmlChar *value, xmlSchemaValPtr *ret, xmlNodePtr node)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValAtomicListNode

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of the predefined list type.

detaileddescription.para: if true a value is computed and returned in ret.

parameterdescription.para: the predefined atomic type for a token in the list

simplesect.para: the number of items if this validates, a negative error code number otherwise

briefdescription.para: Parse an unsigned long into a decimal.

simplesect.para: the number of significant digits in the number or -1 if overflow of the capacity and -2 if it's not a number.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaTypePtr type, const xmlChar *value, xmlSchemaValPtr *val, xmlNodePtr node, int flags, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType ws, int normOnTheFly, int applyNorm, int createStringValue)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValAtomicType

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of the atomic type.

detaileddescription.para: if true a value is computed and returned in val. This checks the value space for list types as well (IDREFS, NMTOKENS).

parameterdescription.para: whether to normalize on the fly
whether to create a string value

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of the predefined type.

detaileddescription.para: if true a value is computed and returned in val.

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of the predefined type.

detaileddescription.para: if true a value is computed and returned in val. This one does apply any normalization to the value.

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of the predefined type.

detaileddescription.para: if true a value is computed and returned in val.

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 decimals.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 durations.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, 2 if x <> y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Makes a copy of v.

detaileddescription.para: The calling program is responsible for freeing the returned value.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a duplicated #xmlSchemaVal or NULL if error.

briefdescription.para: Copies the precomputed value.

detaileddescription.para: This duplicates any string within.

parameterdescription.para: the precomputed value to be copied

simplesect.para: the copy or NULL if a copy for a data-type is not implemented.

briefdescription.para: Compute a new date/time from dt and dur.

detaileddescription.para: This function assumes dt is either #XML_SCHEMAS_DATETIME, #XML_SCHEMAS_DATE, #XML_SCHEMAS_GYEARMONTH, or #XML_SCHEMAS_GYEAR. The returned #xmlSchemaVal is the same type as dt. The calling program is responsible for freeing the returned value.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a new xmlSchemaVal or NULL if error.

briefdescription.para: Normalize dt to GMT time.

detaileddescription.para: The offset parameter is subtracted from the return value is a time-zone offset is present on dt.

parameterdescription.para: an #xmlSchemaVal of a date/time type value.
number of seconds to adjust dt by.

simplesect.para: a normalized copy of dt or NULL if error.

briefdescription.para: Convert mon and year of dt to total number of days.

detaileddescription.para: Take the number of years since (or before) 1 AD and add the number of leap years. This is a function because negative years must be handled a little differently and there is no zero year.

simplesect.para: number of days.

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 date/times.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, 2 if x <> y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 string for their normalized values.

detaileddescription.para: x is a string with whitespace of "preserve", y is a string with a whitespace of "replace". I.e. x could be an "xsd:string" and y an "xsd:normalizedString".

parameterdescription.para: inverts the result if x < y or x > y.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 string for their normalized values.

detaileddescription.para: x is a string with whitespace of "preserve", y is a string with a whitespace of "collapse". I.e. x could be an "xsd:string" and y an "xsd:normalizedString".

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 string for their normalized values.

detaileddescription.para: x is a string with whitespace of "preserve", y is a string with a whitespace of "collapse". I.e. x could be an "xsd:string" and y an "xsd:normalizedString".

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 string for their normalized values.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 string for their normalized values.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 values.

parameterdescription.para: a first float or double value
a second float or double value

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, 2 if x <> y, and -2 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaValType xtype, xmlSchemaValPtr x, const xmlChar *xvalue, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType xws, xmlSchemaValType ytype, xmlSchemaValPtr y, const xmlChar *yvalue, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType yws)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaCompareValuesInternal

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 values.

parameterdescription.para: the first value as a string (optional)
the second value as a string (optional)

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, 2 if x <> y, 3 if not comparable and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 values.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, 2 if x <> y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 values.

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, 2 if x <> y, and -2 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaValType xtype, xmlSchemaValPtr x, const xmlChar *xvalue, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType xws, xmlSchemaValType ytype, xmlSchemaValPtr y, const xmlChar *yvalue, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType yws)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaCompareValuesWhtspExt

briefdescription.para: Compare 2 values.

parameterdescription.para: the first value as a string (optional)
the second value as a string (optional)

simplesect.para: -1 if x < y, 0 if x == y, 1 if x > y, 2 if x <> y, and -2 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Computes the UTF8 length of the normalized value of the string.

simplesect.para: the length or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Extract the value of a facet.

simplesect.para: the value as a long

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaFacet *facet, const xmlChar *value, unsigned long actualLen, unsigned long *expectedLen)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateListSimpleTypeFacet

briefdescription.para: Checks the value of a list simple type against a facet.

parameterdescription.para: the lexical repr of the value to validate
the resulting expected number of list items

simplesect.para: 0 if the value is valid, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of an internal error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaFacetPtr facet, xmlSchemaValType valType, const xmlChar *value, xmlSchemaValPtr val, unsigned long *length, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType ws)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateLengthFacetInternal

briefdescription.para: Checka a value against a "length", "minLength" and "maxLength" facet; sets length to the computed length of value.

parameterdescription.para: the lexical repr. of the value to be validated
the actual length of the value
the whitespace type of the value

simplesect.para: 0 if the value is valid, a positive error code otherwise and -1 in case of an internal or API error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaType *type, xmlSchemaFacet *facet, const xmlChar *value, xmlSchemaVal *val, unsigned long *length)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateLengthFacet

briefdescription.para: Checka a value against a "length", "minLength" and "maxLength" facet; sets length to the computed length of value.

parameterdescription.para: the lexical repr. of the value to be validated
the actual length of the value

simplesect.para: 0 if the value is valid, a positive error code otherwise and -1 in case of an internal or API error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaFacet *facet, xmlSchemaValType valType, const xmlChar *value, xmlSchemaVal *val, unsigned long *length, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType ws)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateLengthFacetWhtsp

briefdescription.para: Checka a value against a "length", "minLength" and "maxLength" facet; sets length to the computed length of value.

parameterdescription.para: the lexical repr. of the value to be validated
the whitespace type of the value
the actual length of the value

simplesect.para: 0 if the value is valid, a positive error code otherwise and -1 in case of an internal or API error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaFacetPtr facet, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType fws, xmlSchemaValType valType, const xmlChar *value, xmlSchemaValPtr val, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType ws)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateFacetInternal

briefdescription.para: Check a value against a facet condition.

parameterdescription.para: the whitespace type of the facet's value
the built-in type of the value
the lexical repr of the value to validate
the whitespace type of the value

simplesect.para: 0 if the element is schemas valid, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Check a value against a facet condition.

parameterdescription.para: the lexical repr of the value to validate

simplesect.para: 0 if the element is schemas valid, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSchemaFacet *facet, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType fws, xmlSchemaValType valType, const xmlChar *value, xmlSchemaVal *val, xmlSchemaWhitespaceValueType ws)
memberdef.name: xmlSchemaValidateFacetWhtsp

briefdescription.para: Check a value against a facet condition.

detaileddescription.para: This takes value normalization according to the specified whitespace types into account. Note that value needs to be the normalized value if the facet is of type "pattern".

parameterdescription.para: the whitespace type of the facet's value
the built-in type of the value
the lexical (or normalized for pattern) repr of the value to validate
the whitespace type of the value

simplesect.para: 0 if the element is schemas valid, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Get the canonical lexical representation of the value.

detaileddescription.para: The caller has to FREE the returned retValue.
WARNING: Some value types are not supported yet, resulting in a retValue of "???".
TODO: XML Schema 1.0 does not define canonical representations for: duration, gYearMonth, gYear, gMonthDay, gMonth, gDay, anyURI, QName, NOTATION. This will be fixed in XML Schema 1.1.

simplesect.para: 0 if the value could be built, 1 if the value type is not supported yet and -1 in case of API errors.

briefdescription.para: Get the canonical representation of the value.

detaileddescription.para: The caller has to free the returned retValue.

parameterdescription.para: the whitespace type of the value

simplesect.para: 0 if the value could be built, 1 if the value type is not supported yet and -1 in case of API errors.

briefdescription.para: Accessor for the type of a value.

simplesect.para: the xmlSchemaValType of the value
